title: United States Postal Service Change of Address (Mail Forwarding)
description: Request that the U.S. Postal Service forward an individual's, family's, or business's mail after a move, permanently or temporarily, using the official Change of Address process (PS Form 3575 / the online Mover's Guide). This document describes the mover type, schedule, old and new addresses, contact details, and identity-verification path. It does not submit the request; the live usps.com source is always authoritative (see VERIFICATION.md).

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored from the official USPS 'Standard Forward Mail' page and the PS Form 3575 / online Mover's Guide structure. Mover type (individual/family/business), permanent vs. temporary forwarding, the 15-day temporary minimum, old/new address, and contact fields were confirmed against the source; individual input fields reflect the form's structure but are not yet independently re-verified item-by-item. Conditional requiredness (name vs. business name by mover type) cannot be expressed in the v0.1 linear flow and is recorded in field descriptions; see GSP-0004. Identity-verification fee and current pricing are intentionally not encoded. Status remains 'draft'. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: moverType
fields.label: Who is moving?
fields.description: individual = one person; family = everyone moving shares the same last name; business = a business is relocating. The choice determines which name fields apply.
fields.sourceRef: Change of Address — Individual / Family / Business

fields.name: changeType
fields.label: Is this move permanent or temporary?
fields.description: permanent = you are moving for good and mail should be rerouted to the new address; temporary = you are relocating for 15 days up to 1 year and mail should be forwarded for a fixed period, then returned to the old address.
fields.sourceRef: Change of Address — Permanent or Temporary

fields.name: firstName
fields.label: First name
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required for an individual or family move; omit for a business move (use businessName instead).

fields.name: middleName
fields.label: Middle name
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: lastName
fields.label: Last name
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required for an individual or family move. For a family move this is the shared household last name; mail is forwarded for everyone with that last name at the old address. Omit for a business move.
fields.sourceRef: Change of Address — Name (family moves share one last name)

fields.name: businessName
fields.label: Business name
fields.description: Required for a business move; omit for an individual or family move.

fields.name: moveEffectiveDate
fields.label: Move effective (start) date
fields.description: The date mail forwarding should begin, YYYY-MM-DD. For PS Form 3575 the Postal Service limits backdating of the start date to no more than 45 days before the submission date.
fields.sourceRef: Change of Address — Move effective date / Start date

fields.name: temporaryEndDate
fields.label: Temporary forwarding end date
fields.description: Required only when changeType is 'temporary': the date forwarding should stop and mail return to the old address, YYYY-MM-DD. Temporary forwarding runs a minimum of 15 days and up to 1 year.
fields.sourceRef: Change of Address — Temporary end date

fields.name: oldAddressLine1
fields.label: Old address — street address
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. The address you are moving from.

fields.name: oldAddressLine2
fields.label: Old address — apartment, suite, or unit (optional)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: oldAddressCity
fields.label: Old address — city
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: oldAddressState
fields.label: Old address — state or territory
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Two-letter USPS state or territory abbreviation, e.g. CA, NY, TX.

fields.name: oldAddressPostalCode
fields.label: Old address — ZIP code
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. 5-digit or ZIP+4 format, e.g. 90210 or 90210-1234.

fields.name: newAddressLine1
fields.label: New address — street address
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. The address you are moving to.

fields.name: newAddressLine2
fields.label: New address — apartment, suite, or unit (optional)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: newAddressCity
fields.label: New address — city
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: newAddressState
fields.label: New address — state or territory
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Two-letter USPS state or territory abbreviation, e.g. CA, NY, TX.

fields.name: newAddressPostalCode
fields.label: New address — ZIP code
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. 5-digit or ZIP+4 format, e.g. 90210 or 90210-1234.

fields.name: phone
fields.label: Phone number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format, e.g. +12025551234.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. A valid email address; used by USPS to send a confirmation. The online process sends a confirmation code to this address.

fields.name: identityVerificationMethod
fields.label: How will you verify your identity?
fields.description: online_card = verify online using a credit or debit card whose billing address matches the old or new address (USPS charges a small identity-verification fee; the current amount is published on the live source); in_person = verify at a Post Office with acceptable photo ID. The fee is intentionally not encoded here.
fields.sourceRef: Change of Address — Identity verification (online card or in-person photo ID)
